pattern: Rounding to the nearest multiple of ten only depends on the last digit: below five rounds down, five or above rounds up, then that rounded amount is simply subtracted from a fixed starting balance.
hints: Only the remainder of the amount when divided by ten decides which way it rounds -- the rest of the number is unaffected.
If that remainder is less than five, drop it to round down; if it is five or more, add enough to reach the next multiple of ten.
Once you have the rounded amount, the answer is just a fixed starting balance of 100 minus that rounded amount.

edge_inputs.description: maximum purchase, already a multiple of ten
remainder above the halfway point rounds up
remainder exactly five rounds upward, not downward
near the top of the range with an exact halfway remainder
remainder nine rounds all the way up to the next ten

stress.description: every purchase amount in the valid range, sampled randomly
